spec_match.rationale: The page now names the non-XSLT parser surfaces directly: DOMParser XML MIME modes, XMLSerializer, XMLHttpRequest.responseXML, fetched XML text, DTD/parameter-entity cases, and the unchanged XSLTProcessor path.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: The concept page now cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5309598397497344 self-heal anchor.

design_system.rationale: The concept uses shared design-system variables for page styling, and the run button sets both background and color.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome/148.0.0.0 at http://localhost:3000/v147/xml-parsing-in-rust-for-non-xslt-scenarios/compatibility-lab/. Before the fix, the concept page had no chromestatus.com/feature/5309598397497344 link and did not mention DTD limits or parameter-entity handling. The Run XML parse tests control produced OK results for application/xml, namespaced XML, image/svg+xml, text/xml, expected parsererror output for malformed XML, and confirmed XSLTProcessor remains the C++ path. After the fix, the page includes a Non-XSLT parser surface section naming DOMParser.parseFromString(), XMLSerializer, XMLHttpRequest.responseXML, fetched XML text, DTD declarations, parameter entities, external entity references, and XSLTProcessor as out of scope. The live test also covers application/xhtml+xml, a DTD parameter entity declaration, and an external entity reference that is not loaded. The required ChromeStatus link and HTML Standard references are present, the concept DOM contains zero raw hex color literals, and /conformance/ ran with 8 pass / 0 fail. Console had no warnings/errors and network requests succeeded.
